title: Decoder-attention command dispatch control PPA
hypothesis: A small central command dispatcher can bound the previously idealized tile/wave command overhead before the Llama7B score32 exp-LUT reduced-replica schedule is promoted as practical.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What Nangate45 PPA and timing cost is introduced by a central attention tile command dispatcher as cluster count and command queue depth scale?
include: generate RTL for central command FIFO and ready/valid command issue
sweep cluster counts 8, 16, and 32 with queue depths 16, 32, and 64
measure Nangate45 area, power, timing, and timing debug paths
keep outputs compact to measure scheduler/control logic before a distributed control network
exclude: distributed per-cluster control fanout and local decode
new Llama7B recost; this is a measured Layer 1 anchor for a dependent L2 consumer

remaining_abstractions: This measures a central command dispatcher with compact dispatch outputs, not the full distributed control network.
The result must be consumed by a later L2 recost before it changes the Llama7B frontier ranking.
The existing command-overhead sensitivity remains the immediate L2 path until this measured control block is available.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure Nangate45 PPA for central decoder-attention command dispatch control blocks at 8, 16, and 32 clusters.

expected_result.reason: This converts a currently idealized scheduler/control term into a measured PPA anchor before later Llama7B recost integration.
